title: Plateau-creator → FUI-switcher manifest transport — how a cross-origin, runtime-authored #747 manifest reaches the FUI-resident #749 switcher
summary: Prep research for decision #887 (the Plateau-creator → FUI-switcher manifest transport, gating the #751 'Your theme' embed capstone, under the Block Explorer epic #746). The #751 capstone wants a 'Your theme' button on the FUI block workbench that opens the embedded Plateau design-system creator (an iframe — Plateau owns the creator, FUI/WE embed it) and feeds the author's freshly-authored #747 manifest to the live #749 switcher so the created theme applies to the real block immediately. The creator (Plateau, cross-origin) and the switcher (FUI, frontierui/workbench/mount.ts) live in DIFFERENT cross-origin iframes. So how does a RUNTIME-AUTHORED manifest cross the Plateau→FUI seam, and should the FUI switcher consume an externally-supplied manifest at all vs only its fixed FUI-resident gallery? This is the explicit CROSS-ORIGIN, RUNTIME-AUTHORED COUSIN of #881. #881 ruled the same-origin, static case (WE hands server-emitted nav/brand/footer to a FUI mode-C mount in WE's own DOM) and landed on DOM-mediated data on the mount point — because same-origin DOM is readable and the config is build-time. #887 flips BOTH distinguishing properties: cross-origin (Plateau iframe ↔ FUI iframe) and runtime (user-authored in the creator) — so the same-origin policy blocks all cross-frame DOM/contentDocument reads, and a cooperating message channel is the only carrier. The rhyme resolves OPPOSITELY on the channel: #881 → DOM-data (same-origin + static); #887 → postMessage (cross-origin + runtime). GROUNDING THE REAL TREES: (1) An origin-validated postMessage channel ALREADY EXISTS in the FUI embed SDK — embed-host.ts installs a message router that rejects any message whose event.origin ≠ the frame's validated origin (embed-host.ts:142), matches event.source to a live contentWindow, and sends host→guest via postToGuest targeted at the validated origin; embed-guest.ts posts to its embedder's origin and origin-gates inbound; the wire contract (contract.ts) is a versioned EmbedEnvelope (channel:'fui-embed', v:1), a GuestMessage|HostMessage union, an isEmbedMessage guard, and an envelope() helper — already an extensible origin-validated message bus (ready/resize/overlay-open/overlay-close/init), so adding an apply-design-system message type is a contract ADDITION, not a new channel. #887-A is NOT greenfield. (2) The switcher's apply path is one already-factored function — frontierui/workbench/mount.ts:277 applyDesignSystem(preset) sets themeTokens as custom properties, sets data-intent-*/data-trait-*, re-renders the same block with no code change; the gallery wires each chip to it. Consuming an external manifest = build a DesignSystemPreset from the payload and call the EXISTING applyDesignSystem(). (3) The transported manifest must be the RESOLVED/INLINE #747 form, not a DTCG-path manifest — the workbench's presets carry themeTokens resolved inline as a {'--custom-prop': value} map ('because the workbench has no WE↔FUI channel … it consumes the #747 manifest shape with its own preset data'); the WE catalog form (designSystems.json) uses manifest path refs, and cross-origin the FUI switcher CANNOT dereference a themeTokens path on a Plateau/WE origin (no channel + CORS), so the creator must serialize the FULLY-RESOLVED manifest (token values inlined) — #887's analogue of #881's serializable-ChromeConfig constraint. TOPOLOGY constrained by #809 (no WE↔FUI channel): the FUI workbench hosts the Plateau creator as a NESTED CHILD iframe (the sanctioned #746 'embedded Plateau configurators' pattern), making creator→switcher a DIRECT Plateau↔FUI parent-child message; the sibling-iframe alternative (WE page hosts both, relays between them) reconstitutes exactly the WE↔FUI channel #809 forbids — EXCLUDED, not weighed. PRIOR-ART FAMILIES for a cross-iframe runtime handoff: (1) postMessage (runtime push, ephemeral) — the platform-standard cross-origin channel and dominant embedded-editor→live-preview pattern (Stripe Elements, Figma/CodeSandbox/StackBlitz embeds, Storybook manager↔preview), always origin-validated, serializable payload, no backend/persistence/reload → the live-apply channel; (2) persist-to-backend + fetch-by-id (durable) — the share/persist pattern (Tokens Studio Pro sync, zeroheight/Supernova), and per #775 the PAID tier (hosted persistence), right for save-and-share but wrong as the live-apply channel (drags a free zero-friction loop behind hosted infra + a round-trip); (3) URL/navigation handoff (encode + reload) — WE already has the serialization in #754, but #754 encodes a manifest REF not inline tokens and a reload-per-tweak destroys live authoring. MAPPED ONTO #887: Fork 1 (live-apply transport) — family 1 = Option A (origin-validated postMessage over the existing FUI embed channel + an apply-design-system message + the Plateau creator's trusted origin), family 2 = Option B (persist+fetch — the paid share path, a structural defect as the live channel), family 3 = Option C (URL/#754 reload — share/deep-link, breaks live iteration) → A holds, strengthened by the existing origin-validated channel being directly reusable. Forced invariants the survey hardened (not forks): Plateau→FUI direct + WE never brokers (#809); the switcher consumes the runtime manifest (fixed-gallery-only is the broken branch — severs #751's loop; injection point is the existing applyDesignSystem() at mount.ts:277); the wire shape is the resolved/inline manifest (token values inlined). Full grounding in reports/2026-06-17-887-plateau-creator-fui-switcher-transport.md.
